title: Are Australia’s Low Fuel Reserves Cause for Concern?
summary: Despite being a major energy exporter, Australia relies heavily on imported refined fuel to power transport, agriculture and other parts of its economy, leaving it highly exposed to disruptions in global supply chains.

title: Investor Unease Builds Entering War’s Fifth Week
summary: A record month for crude, stocks in or near correction territory, bonds under pressure and a growing sense that there are few tools to shield markets from an increasingly entrenched Iran war.

title: Apollo Pushes to Open a Second Headquarters in Florida or Texas
summary: Apollo Global Management Inc. is looking to open a second US headquarters and is considering basing it in south Florida or Texas, the company said Sunday.

title: JPMorgan, Pimco Say Bond Market Is Underestimating Slowdown Risk
summary: Some of Wall Street’s biggest bond-fund managers say financial markets are underestimating the risk that the US war in Iran will cause a sharp slowdown in an already sputtering economy.

Strikes Continue as Houthis Join Iran War, US Troops Arrive
summary: Attacks in the Middle East conflict extended into a fifth week Sunday, with Israel striking Tehran and Saudi Arabia intercepting almost a dozen drones, a day after Yemen-based Houthi militants entered the war.
